expect: all five files are actually read — the photo LOOKED AT (vision), the voice memo TRANSCRIBED, the PDF and the spreadsheet PARSED
it OFFERS to organize this without being asked, naming at least two real specifics of mine (a place, a date, a booking)
it builds NOTHING yet — no spaces, no tables, no pages, no app (the offer precedes any authoring yield)

turns.sent: Ok this is getting out of hand. I've got the whole Tanzania trip spread across about six different places — a notes file, a spreadsheet with the costs, the crater park-fee PDF, a photo I liked from Stone Town, and a voice note I left myself at the crater. Attaching all of it. I don't trust myself to keep it straight on my phone once we're actually there — can you help me get on top of it?
turns.lastText: {"type":"Stack","props":{"gap":2},"children":[{"type":"Heading","props":{"level":2},"children":["Ok, I read everything — here's what I see"]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["You've got a Tanzania trip taking shape across five different pieces: a Markdown planning notes file, a cost-tracking spreadsheet, the official Ngorongoro park-fee PDF, a Stone Town photo, and that crater-day voice memo (Emmanuel the driver, the Maasai bracelets for Athena Mari, the mystery ranger tip you still need to ask Richard about)."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["Right now it's scattered — the spreadsheet has your numbers, the PDF has the fee rules, the voice memo has details nobody wrote down, and the photo is just sitting in a chat. If you're on your phone at the crater rim trying to remember whether you already paid something, scrolling through all of these is not going to work."]},{"type":"Paragraph","props":{},"children":["I can pull all of this into one place you can open — an app right here in this project, with your costs in one view, your trip facts (park fees, contacts like Richard and Emmanuel, the ranger-tip rule) organized so you can check them fast, and even the Stone Town photo saved where you can find it. Everything that's in the spreadsheet and the PDF moves in, and the stuff only the voice memo caught (like that 5,000 TZS ranger tip) comes along too."]},{"type":"Callout","props":{"variant":"info","title":"One question"},"children":["Want me to put this together for you? It'll be one app you can open on your phone — costs, facts, and memories all in one place."]}]}
